2606.23136.title: LLM-Aided A* Search in Non-Geometric Network Graphs
2606.23136.paper_summary: Finding the shortest path in non-geometric network graphs, where edge weights encode arbitrary metrics such as latency or monetary cost rather than spatial distance, poses a challenge for informed search algorithms. Their efficiency depends on an informative heuristic, typically supplied in spatial domains by geometric distances that have no counterpart on non-geometric graphs. We propose a large language model (LLM)-aided A* algorithm in which an LLM generates intermediate waypoints that guide the A* expansion toward promising graph regions. At the core of the approach are landmark distances, which serve both as an admissible landmark-based (ALT) heuristic for the search and as a compact structural feature that, supplied to the LLM, restores the distance-to-destination signal it would otherwise lack on non-geometric graphs. Our comprehensive experiments on multiple graph topologies with up to 2,000 nodes demonstrate that LLM-generated waypoints reduce the number of expanded nodes by around 50% while incurring only a marginal path cost increase compared to the optimal solution. We further analyze the impact of prompt engineering and show that incorporating compact structural features, namely heuristic estimates, is more effective than advanced prompting techniques. These findings demonstrate the potential of combining LLM- based guidance with classical search algorithms for efficient network optimization.
2606.23136.paper_summary_zh: 在非幾何網路圖中尋找最短路徑，其中邊權重編碼了任意度量，例如延遲或金錢成本，而不是空間距離，對於知識搜尋演算法來說是一個挑戰。它們的效率依賴於一個具資訊性的啟發式，通常在空間領域由幾何距離提供，而在非幾何圖中則沒有對應的概念。我們提出了一種大型語言模型（LLM）輔助的 A* 演算法，其中 LLM 生成中間路徑點，以指導 A* 向有前景的圖區域擴展。該方法的核心是地標距離，這既作為搜尋的可接受地標基（ALT）啟發式，也作為一個緊湊的結構特徵，提供給 LLM，恢復了它在非幾何圖中缺失的目的地距離信號。我們在多個圖拓撲上進行的全面實驗，節點數量高達 2,000，顯示 LLM 生成的路徑點將擴展的節點數量減少了約 50%，同時與最佳解相比，僅產生了微小的路徑成本增加。我們進一步分析了提示工程的影響，並顯示納入緊湊結構特徵，即啟發式估計，比高級提示技術更有效。這些發現展示了將基於 LLM 的指導與經典搜尋演算法相結合以實現高效網路優化的潛力。
2606.23136.author: Nouf Alabbasi et.al.
2606.23136.authors: Nouf Alabbasi, Esraa Ghourab, Omar Alhussein
